_doc: Reference catalogue of music-notation glyphs and how each maps to our compact note format (Pitch+Octave(Duration), e.g. C5(8), R(16), D#4(q.)). `compact` is the AI-friendly handle: how the glyph shows up in a bar's note string. `smufl` is the Standard Music Font Layout name. Status 'known' = catalogued; user-added unidentified glyphs live in the data dir with status 'unidentified'.

glyphs.name: Treble (G) clef
glyphs.meaning: Pitches read in the treble register; our melody voice.

glyphs.name: Bass (F) clef
glyphs.meaning: Pitches read in the bass register; our bass voice. NB: in some passages the left hand is written in a treble clef and plays high.

glyphs.name: Filled notehead
glyphs.compact: any pitched token with duration q or shorter, e.g. C5(8)
glyphs.meaning: A quarter note or shorter (duration set by stem/flags/beams).

glyphs.name: Hollow notehead (with stem)
glyphs.meaning: A half note (2 quarter-beats).

glyphs.name: Whole note
glyphs.meaning: A whole note (4 quarter-beats); no stem.

glyphs.name: Stem
glyphs.meaning: Vertical line on a notehead; presence means quarter or shorter. Stem direction is engraving, not pitch.

glyphs.name: Eighth flag
glyphs.meaning: One flag on an unbeamed stem = eighth note.

glyphs.name: Sixteenth flag
glyphs.meaning: Two flags = sixteenth note.

glyphs.name: Single beam
glyphs.meaning: One beam joining stems = eighth notes.

glyphs.name: Double beam
glyphs.meaning: Two beams = sixteenth notes (the Für Elise runs).

glyphs.name: Triple beam
glyphs.meaning: Three beams = thirty-second notes (fast run-backs).

glyphs.name: Augmentation dot
glyphs.meaning: Dot to the right of a notehead/rest = 1.5x its duration.

glyphs.name: Whole rest
glyphs.meaning: Silence for a whole note (hangs under a staff line).

glyphs.name: Half rest
glyphs.meaning: Silence for a half note (sits on a staff line).

glyphs.name: Quarter rest
glyphs.meaning: Silence for a quarter note (the squiggle).

glyphs.name: Eighth rest
glyphs.meaning: Silence for an eighth (one hook).

glyphs.name: Sixteenth rest
glyphs.meaning: Silence for a sixteenth (two hooks). Common after the eighth in the Für Elise answer figure.

glyphs.name: Thirty-second rest
glyphs.meaning: Silence for a thirty-second (three hooks).

glyphs.name: Sharp
glyphs.compact: # after the letter, e.g. D#5, G#3
glyphs.meaning: Raise the pitch a semitone (D# is the Für Elise signature note).

glyphs.name: Flat
glyphs.compact: b after the letter, e.g. Bb4
glyphs.meaning: Lower the pitch a semitone (Bb appears in the F-major B-section).

glyphs.name: Natural
glyphs.compact: plain letter (cancels a prior #/b in the bar)
glyphs.meaning: Cancel a previous accidental.

glyphs.name: Common time (C = 4/4)
glyphs.meaning: The 'C' glyph = 4/4 (four quarter-beats). A 4/4 bar = 16 sixteenths. Mozart K.545 mvt I (Allegro).

glyphs.name: Time signature 2/4
glyphs.meaning: Two quarter-beats per bar. A 2/4 bar = 8 sixteenths. Mozart K.545 mvt III (Rondo).

glyphs.name: Time signature 3/4
glyphs.meaning: Three quarter-beats per bar. A 3/4 bar = 12 sixteenths. Mozart K.545 mvt II (Andante).

glyphs.name: Time signature 3/8
glyphs.meaning: Three eighth-beats per bar. A 3/8 bar = 6 sixteenths (1.5 quarter-beats). Beethoven Für Elise.

glyphs.name: Key signature
glyphs.compact: job/bar meta key, e.g. A minor
glyphs.meaning: Sharps/flats applied throughout; drives the key-fit check.

glyphs.name: Barline
glyphs.meaning: Measure boundary. Detected mechanically to segment systems into bars.

glyphs.name: Final barline
glyphs.meaning: Thin+thick line ending the piece.

glyphs.name: Repeat barline
glyphs.compact: (not yet modelled — expand the repeat when transcribing)
glyphs.meaning: Two dots + thick line: repeat the enclosed section.

glyphs.name: Volta bracket (1. / 2.)
glyphs.compact: (not yet modelled — take the through-path / 2nd ending)
glyphs.meaning: First/second endings for a repeat.

glyphs.name: Brace
glyphs.meaning: Joins the treble+bass staves into one piano grand staff.

glyphs.name: Tie
glyphs.meaning: Curved line joining two notes of the SAME pitch into one sustained note.

glyphs.name: Slur
glyphs.meaning: Curved line over DIFFERENT pitches = play legato.

glyphs.name: Ledger line
glyphs.meaning: Short line extending the staff for notes above/below it (e.g. the E6 in the consequent).

glyphs.name: Pianissimo (pp)
glyphs.meaning: Very soft. Für Elise opens pp.

glyphs.name: Piano (p)
glyphs.meaning: Soft. Mozart K.545 mvt I opens p (p dolce).

glyphs.name: Mezzo-forte (mf)
glyphs.meaning: Medium-loud. Mozart K.545 mvt III (Rondo) opens mf.

glyphs.name: Fortissimo (ff)
glyphs.meaning: Very loud.

glyphs.name: Sustain pedal (Ped. / *)
glyphs.meaning: Press (Ped.) / release (*) the sustain pedal.

glyphs.name: Staccato dot
glyphs.meaning: Dot above/below a notehead = shorten/detach (NOT an augmentation dot, which is to the right).

glyphs.name: Fermata
glyphs.meaning: Hold the note longer than written.
